danger_signals.meaning: Burai faction combatants; immediate threat requiring destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen boundary moving in cardinal directions (left, right, up, down)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state pressure; player death if trapped on edge—conveys hostile environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinct sprite at level terminus
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; must be defeated to progress; marks territory control by Burai

danger_signals.visual_form: High enemy count on-screen causing visual flicker
danger_signals.meaning: Overwhelming threat density; system stress visual indicator of danger intensity

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible objects (ring, laser, missile weapon upgrades)
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; progression toward capability to overcome Burai threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Side-screen gaps revealing alternate chambers with additional power-ups
safety_signals.meaning: Safe exploration zones; reward for precise timing and spatial awareness

safety_signals.visual_form: Basic cannon → ring → laser → missile (increasing visual impact/brightness)
safety_signals.meaning: Increasing player safety margin; visual feedback of growing combat capability
Progression security; reduces threat of total loss

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cyborg/robot aesthetic; half-animal hybrid troop design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Seven distinct bases across galaxy, each producing themed Burai troops with animal-hybrid characteristics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Seven occupied facilities/bases serving as level stages; mechanized, industrial base aesthetics implied by robot production centers

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Proton suit; protagonist with laser gun—individualized fighter identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traveling through space between Burai bases; traversing contested galaxy territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Seven industrial bases scattered across galaxy; robot production facilities occupy space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous spawn of enemy troops; screen scrolls through claimed territory; boss entities guard installations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High enemy density on-screen; scrolling direction changes unpredictably (cardinal directions); varied background graphics reflecting different galactic locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies pursue and fire; screen pressure (edge-death mechanic) creates dynamic threat; directional scrolling prevents static defensive positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite appears at level terminus; distinct larger scale compared to standard troops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss must be defeated to proceed; marks conquest of one of seven Burai production centers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen-edge gaps revealing alternate chambers; power-up clusters visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional exploration; timing-based entry mechanic; fewer secret rooms on higher difficulties indicate increasing threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon visual upgrades (ring, laser, missile) with increasing brightness/impact appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Power-ups fundamentally alter combat effectiveness; visual hierarchy signals player progression through Burai threat levels

environmental_storytelling.example: Seven bases distributed across galaxy geography; each level represents assault on singular Burai production facility; moving through space toward liberation of universe
Higher difficulties (Albatross, Ace, Ultimate) reduce secret room availability and tighten timing—conveys escalating Burai defensive posture

environmental_storytelling.meaning: Communicates distinct Burai facility locations; each base occupies unique galactic territory; visual differentiation prevents monotony while reinforcing multi-front war narrative

environmental_storytelling.example: Half-robot, half-animal hybrid design across all Burai troops establishes faction genetic/production signature; visual unity across seven bases despite geographic separation
Screen scrolls in multiple cardinal directions within single level; changes direction mid-stage; forces constant player repositioning—conveys Burai territory is labyrinthine and hostile, not static

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spawn continuously; pursue and fire on player; density increases threat perception
Stationary or patterned movement; requires sustained fire to defeat; marks base seizure on victory
Increased weapon range/impact; can traverse more aggressively; visually distinct from base state
Weak offensive capability; vulnerable to high-density enemy fire; must prioritize evasion and power-up acquisition
Inaccessible; requires precise screen-edge timing to enter; rewards exploratory risk-taking

notes: Burai Fighter communicates a totalitarian occupation narrative through visual and mechanical language. The Burai faction's presence is established through seven scattered production bases and continuous troop generation, creating a sense of overwhelming systematic threat. The protagonist's journey is conveyed as a solo desperate assault on organized military infrastructure. Power-up progression (basic cannon → ring → laser → missile) serves as both mechanical escalation and visual confidence indicator—the protagonist visibly becomes more dangerous as they consume enemy territory. The screen-edge death mechanic is a critical world-state communicator: the game literally will not let the player remain static; safety is only achieved through forward progress, reinforcing that the universe is actively hostile and Burai dominance is not a passive state but an aggressive ongoing process. The variable directional scrolling prevents players from learning static defensive positions—each base is topologically unique. Secret rooms on difficulty scaling (fewer on harder modes) indicate that higher Burai alert levels reduce opportunities for reprieve.
